summary:
The incorrect choice pertains to all intrinsic muscles being exclusively controlled via the recurrent laryngeal nerve; instead, some also rely partially or fully through additional nerves such as branches originating directly at spinal cord levels higher than where RLN originates and courses down during embryogenesis/development stages post-natally.
The claim asserting exclusive control over specific intrinsic larynges’ musculature by a single cranial nerve fails due diverse neural supply patterns observed clinically & anatomic studies reviewed here today.
 Agent1's final summary focuses more broadly rather than strictly adhering to two-sentence constraints while providing detailed explanations which may be unnecessary based purely off requirement
